extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_b is set true only by r1 (condition: g6pd_deficiency). rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (condition: the conjunction of all ten flags, which includes g6pd_deficiency). Critically, r2's condition is a superset of r1's condition. Priorities resolve conflicts only within the same output, but r1 writes rec_drug_b while r2 writes rec_drug_a — different outputs — so they never compete; both can take effect simultaneously. Whenever r2 fires, g6pd_deficiency is necessarily true, so r1 also fires. Setting all ten flags in r2's condition to true makes rec_drug_a := true (via r2) and rec_drug_b := true (via r1), with rec_drug_c falling to its default false (ckd_dialysis = false). This reachable input yields rec_drug_a ∧ rec_drug_b = true, violating mutual exclusion. The property is violated.
